suggested_next_actions: Fix the cooldown/state reload bug in `codex-watchdog.py` so the post-HALT cooldown is actually persisted and decremented across later Stops.
Make the state lock cover the full read-modify-write transition, or rebase mutations onto the latest on-disk state before saving, so concurrent Stop hooks cannot clobber each other.

findings.title: Post-HALT cooldown is lost after the state reload
findings.explanation: The code decrements `state["cooldown_remaining"]` before releasing the lock, but then immediately replaces `state` with `load_state(state_path)` after the Codex call. That throws away the decremented value, so the subsequent `save_state()` writes the pre-decrement cooldown back to disk. After the first HALT, the cooldown will stay pinned instead of counting down, which breaks AC4 and the intended WARN-capping decay.
findings.recommendation: Carry the decremented cooldown forward when you reacquire the lock, or keep the lock across the full read-modify-write sequence so the mutation is not discarded by the reload.

findings.title: The new file lock still allows lost updates under concurrency
findings.explanation: The lock is released during the Codex call, and after reacquiring it the code reloads state but does not merge against the earlier snapshot. If two Stop hooks overlap, both can analyze the same input and whichever one saves last can overwrite the other's `recent_wakes`, topic counts, or cooldown changes. That means the race from the prior review is not actually eliminated; it is only narrowed.
findings.recommendation: Either hold the lock for the whole state transition, or implement a reload-and-merge/compare-and-swap retry before saving so concurrent Stop hooks cannot clobber each other.

verdict.rationale: The `/watchdog off` fix and the HALT integration test look correct, but the state-lock change introduced a regression: the cooldown decrement is discarded after the reload, and the lock still does not fully serialize concurrent state updates.
